title: stipa × zaissanica
intro: Stipa × zaissanica, a perennial grass of the Poaceae family, is a hybrid species resulting from the cross between Stipa capillata and Stipa zaissanica. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory problems and promote overall health. Stipa × zaissanica is often used to make herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and teas, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal benefits.
